commands.repair_reason: Contributor-owned real behavior proof is missing and the remaining blocker is an approval parsing security boundary that needs maintainer review before merge.; Needs attention: The diff broadens approval-wrapper parsing while an existing consumer feeds the parser generic command output.; Review finding: [P2] Gate approval parsing to trusted lifecycle data — src/agents/exec-approval-result.ts:88 (sha=2409ba561334f741106860bf16226e60df43bdd5)
commands.review_summary:
The PR replaces regex-based exec approval result parsing with balanced metadata parsing, changes one gateway denial reason literal to colon-separated text, strips parentheses from node exec denial reasons, adds focused tests, and adds a changelog entry.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main emits nested `approval-timeout (allowlist-miss)` metadata and the current `[^)]*` parser rejects that denied payload. I did not establish a live Telegram/Gateway reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Contributor-owned real behavior proof is missing and the remaining blocker is an approval parsing security boundary that needs maintainer review before merge.

target.title: fix(exec): parse nested approval metadata in async followups
target.body:
## Summary
- replace the fragile exec approval metadata regex with a small balanced-parentheses parser for `Exec denied (...)` and `Exec finished (...)` payloads
- add nested-parentheses coverage for denied and finished parsing plus safe denied copy generation
- add followup-path tests proving nested denial payloads stay on the denied branch instead of the generic completion branch

## Root cause
`parseExecApprovalResultText()` used `^exec denied \(([^)]*)\):...$`, which stops at the first `)` inside metadata. Real payloads like `approval-timeout (allowlist-miss)` therefore failed the denied match and fell through to the generic completed/other followup handling.

## Notes
- Checked current `upstream/main` (`4a3030df9e`) before implementing; the nested-parentheses parser bug is still present there.
- This is related to #72148: that PR removes a leaked resume-failed prefix, while this change fixes the underlying denied-result misclassification.

## Test plan
- `pnpm exec vitest run src/agents/exec-approval-result.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-approval-followup.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/agents/exec-approval-result.ts src/agents/exec-approval-result.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-approval-followup.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/agents/exec-approval-result.ts src/agents/exec-approval-result.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-approval-followup.test.ts`
target.author: amittell
